item.title: N.J. man says he was almost kicked off plane over message on T-shirt
item.description: A New Jersey man says his T-shirt nearly got him kicked off a United Airlines flight.

item.title: Astronomers discover sugar in space that's also found in raspberries
item.description: The sugar, called erythrulose, lurks in what's called the interstellar medium: thin clouds of gas and dust littered between stars.

item.title: 6 mistakes that can make debt relief more expensive
item.description: Debt relief can save you thousands if you approach it right, but a few common missteps could erase those savings.

item.title: 1 person killed in Maine shooting involving ICE, state House speaker says
item.description: A person was killed during a shooting in Maine involving U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement on Monday morning, state House Speaker Ryan Fecteau said.

item.title: Georgia families face losing their homes to make way for AI data centers: "It's theft"
item.description: Some families in Georgia are being forced to sell their homes or face government seizures to make way for AI data centers. CBS News' Skyler Henry has more.

item.title: Trump says U.S. to "keep" Strait of Hormuz, impose 20% fee on cargo
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. will "probably run" the Strait of Hormuz, as dueling attacks with Iran continue over control of the strategic waterway.

item.title: Trump backs Lindsey Graham's sister to finish Senate term
item.description: President Trump recommended that South Carolina's governor appoint the late Sen. Lindsey Graham's sister Darline to serve out the rest of his term in the Senate.

item.title: South Carolina governor to announce Lindsey Graham's replacement Monday
item.description: South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster will hold a press conference to announce his appointment to fill the vacancy left by Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.

item.title: Lindsey Graham "wasn't afraid to do what he thought was right," Lt. Gov. Evette says
item.description: South Carolina Lt. Gov. Pamela Evette joined "CBS News 24/7 Mornings" to reflect on Sen. Lindsey Graham's impact after his death.

item.title: Oil prices rise as the U.S. and Iran fight over control of Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil prices rose on Monday as the conflict between the U.S. and Iran intensified days after President Trump declared the countries' ceasefire agreement to be over. Iran claimed the Strait of Hormuz was closed as both sides exchanged more strikes over the weekend. Kristin Myers, ETF editor-in-chief at ASSET TV, joins CBS News to share her predictions.

item.title: At least 27 killed in Bangkok beer hall fire, authorities say
item.description: Video shared by first responders​ shows a huge blaze, with flames coming out of the front door of the Na Ladprao bar in the northern part of the Thai capital.

item.title: Egg producers reach federal and state settlement after price-fixing probe
item.description: Three U.S. egg producers will be required to provide 53 million eggs to food banks and to pay a $3.3 million financial penalty.

item.title: Videos show aftermath of deadly Bangkok bar fire
item.description: At least 27 people were killed after a fire broke out at a beer hall in Bangkok, Thailand. CBS News' Anna Coren has the latest.

item.title: Iran and U.S. both claim control over Strait of Hormuz, trade more strikes
item.description: Iran and the U.S. have both claimed control over the Strait of Hormuz after trading more strikes over the weekend and into Monday. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has the latest.

item.title: Trump says U.S. reinstating Iran blockade in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Trump says the U.S. will be known as "THE GUARDIAN OF THE HORMUZ STRAIT," and will charge 20% on all cargo shipped via the waterway to cover security costs.

item.title: Breaking down Lindsey Graham's key accomplishments
item.description: Tributes are pouring in for longtime South Carolina Sen. Lindsey Graham, who died Saturday at 71 from an aortic dissection, according to the medical examiner's preliminary findings. CBS News' Fin Gomez breaks down some of his key accomplishments.

item.title: "Dear David": Pep talk box, cleaning up sidewalks and reuniting lost pets with their owners
item.description: In this week's "Beg-Knows America," CBS News contributor David Begnaud shares three stories of people helping to make a difference. A Seattle woman spreads hope with a pep talk box. Plus, a Gen Z New Yorker cleans up the city one sidewalk at a time and a veteran uses a drone to reunite lost pets with their families.

item.title: British counterterrorism police lead probe into murder of politician
item.description: British counterterrorism police are now leading the investigation into the death of Ann Widdecombe after "new information and evidence" came to light.

item.title: 5 debt collection rules to know after a loved one dies
item.description: Knowing what debt collectors can and can't do after a borrower dies could protect your family from costly mistakes.

item.title: Find your perfect match during a World Cup match
item.description: The World Cup is not only bringing people together for the love of soccer, but some are turning watch parties into a chance to make a real connection. Maria Avgitidis, a professional matchmaker and founder of Agape, shares tips on how to use the tournament to kick off a new romance.

item.title: What is the travel trend "set-jetting?"
item.description: Henley Vazquez, a seasoned travel adviser and co-founder of the travel agency Fora, speaks to "CBS Mornings" about one of the biggest travel trends: "set-jetting." It's when travelers seek destinations that are featured in TV shows and movies.

item.title: Chronic inflammation could be impacting your mental and cognitive health, research shows
item.description: Chronic inflammation, characterized by symptoms like fatigue, muscle aches and headaches, has also been linked to cancer, high blood pressure and more. Now, research suggests it may also be affecting your mental and cognitive health. CBS News contributor Dr. Mark Hyman gives tips on how to combat inflammation.

item.title: Trump praises Lindsey Graham for his bipartisan efforts, global impact
item.description: President Trump and other U.S. lawmakers paid tribute to Sen. Lindsey Graham after the South Carolina Republican senator died Saturday. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns has more.

item.title: Congress returns with long to-do list as Graham's death hangs over Capitol
item.description: Congress is returning to Washington with limited time to address a number of priorities ahead of a lengthy August recess and the sprint to the midterm elections.

item.title: Bison tosses man 8 feet in the air at Yellowstone, video shows
item.description: A 65-year-old man was thrown 8 feet into the air by bison who charged at him in Yellowstone National Park, video shows.

item.title: Sam Neill, "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano" star, dies at 78
item.description: New Zealand actor Sam Neill, known for "Jurassic Park" and "The Piano," died Monday at 78, his family says.

item.title: Aircraft goes down in reservoir while fighting fire; 1 body recovered
item.description: A body was recovered from a firefighting aircraft that went down in Silver Jack Reservoir in western Colorado.

item.title: Wildfire of "exceptional scale" burning near Paris
item.description: The wildfire is piling pressure on a region facing its third heat wave since May.

item.title: Changing conditions cause expanded evacuation orders for Ferris Fire
item.description: Colorado officials expanded mandatory evacuation orders for residents near the Ferris Fire as conditions continued to change on Sunday.

item.title: The controversy over qualified immunity
item.description: The doctrine whereby police officers are protected from civil lawsuits in certain cases has come under question – as citizens who claim their Constitutional rights had been violated by police have been mired in legal actions for years
